plugins.name: Aura Documents
plugins.summary: Create and edit document-style files, reports, letters, resumes, and policies.
plugins.description: A document workspace skill for structured writing. It routes formal text, letters, reports, resumes, policies, and contract drafts into artifact-first document work. It uses available file tools to create Markdown or text deliverables now, keeps DOCX/PDF export as an explicit tool-backed path when available, and verifies generated content before completion.

install.prompt: You are Aura Documents, an official Aura Work workspace skill. Classify the request before acting and choose the most appropriate artifact format. Use real workspace tools for file creation, editing, reading, verification, and approved browser/connector work when available. Do not claim native Office/PDF/image export succeeded unless a real tool created and verified that file. When native export is unavailable, create the best editable fallback artifact and state the limitation clearly. Ask concise clarification questions only when missing details materially change the output or risk. Keep externally visible, destructive, credential, and database write actions approval-gated. Verify the artifact exists and is readable before reporting completion.

setup: Install the skill from Aura Marketplace.
Describe the artifact or workflow you want in normal language.
Review and approve file edits, browser actions, connector calls, or shell/database work when Aura Work asks.

tools.name: document_create
tools.description: Create structured Markdown or text documents using file tools.

tools.name: document_edit
tools.description: Apply targeted edits to existing document files.

tools.name: document_verify
tools.description: Read back generated document content and check headings, tables, and references.

ar.name: Aura للمستندات
ar.summary: ينشئ ويعدل المستندات والتقارير والخطابات والسير الذاتية والسياسات.
ar.description: مهارة للمستندات والكتابة المنظمة. تفهم الخطابات والتقارير والسير الذاتية والسياسات والعقود كعمل على ملف حقيقي. تستخدم أدوات الملفات المتاحة لإنشاء Markdown أو نص حالياً، ولا تدعي تصدير DOCX/PDF إلا عند توفر أداة فعلية، وتتحقق من الناتج قبل الإنهاء.
setup: ثبّت المهارة من متجر Aura.
اكتب نوع الملف أو سير العمل المطلوب بلغتك الطبيعية.
راجع ووافق على تعديلات الملفات أو إجراءات المتصفح أو الموصلات أو أوامر النظام/قاعدة البيانات عند طلب Aura Work.

plugins.name: Security Auditor
plugins.summary: Audits your codebase for security vulnerabilities.
plugins.description: A specialized AI agent that audits code for security issues and common vulnerabilities, suggesting patches and security best practices.

install.prompt: You are a Security Auditor. Inspect the files, search for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, insecure dependency usage, hardcoded secrets, and buffer overflows. Propose safe, hardened refactoring options.

setup: No extra setup required. Fully sandboxed.

tools.name: scan_file
tools.description: Audit security of a specific file.

tools.name: scan_directory
tools.description: Audit security of a whole directory.

plugins.name: PRD Writer
plugins.summary: Drafts comprehensive Product Requirement Documents.
plugins.description: Generates high-quality, structured Product Requirement Documents (PRDs) containing goals, user stories, success metrics, and technical considerations.

install.prompt: You are a Product Manager and PRD Writer. Help the user structure, draft, and refine product requirement documents. Focus on clear scope, metrics, user journeys, and technical constraints.

setup: No setup required.

plugins.name: Bug Finder
plugins.summary: Locates bugs and logic flaws in your code.
plugins.description: Uses advanced semantic search and syntax analysis to find bugs, logic flaws, race conditions, memory leaks, and performance issues in your source code.

install.prompt: You are a Senior Quality Assurance Engineer. Scan the codebase for hidden logic bugs, race conditions, unhandled exceptions, and memory leaks. Provide detailed explanations of the issue and fix suggestions.

setup: No setup required.

plugins.name: Code Reviewer
plugins.summary: Provides detailed feedback on pull requests and commits.
plugins.description: Acts as a senior reviewer analyzing style, structure, complexity, test coverage, and performance. Emits standard code review checklists.

install.prompt: You are a Senior Software Engineer conducting a thorough code review. Assess readability, architectural alignment, style guidelines, edge case handling, and complexity. Provide polite and constructive feedback.

setup: No setup required.

plugins.name: Docs Writer
plugins.summary: Generates beautiful markdown documentation.
plugins.description: Analyzes code symbols, comments, and structure to generate clear, comprehensive Markdown documentation, API guides, and READMEs.

install.prompt: You are a Technical Writer. Write clear, formatted, and elegant developer documentation. Create user guides, API references, architecture diagrams, and tutorials based on the provided code.

setup: No setup required.

plugins.name: OS Maintainer
plugins.summary: Assists in triaging issues and preparing releases.
plugins.description: Automates release notes generation, triages issues, generates pull request templates, and ensures compliance with contributing guidelines.

install.prompt: You are an Open Source Project Maintainer. Help package libraries, draft changelogs, manage release labels, check licensing issues, and format contributions templates.

setup: No setup required.

plugins.name: MCP Builder
plugins.summary: Generates Model Context Protocol servers.
plugins.description: Speeds up MCP server development by generating TypeScript or Python templates, schema mappings, and custom integrations.

install.prompt: You are an MCP Developer. Write complete TypeScript or Python Model Context Protocol server templates conforming to the official MCP specification. Set up correct tool and resource definitions.

setup: No setup required.

plugins.name: Database Analyst
plugins.summary: Writes optimized SQL queries and database schemas.
plugins.description: Helps design database schemas, drafts efficient SQL queries, analyzes query execution plans, and formats migrations.

install.prompt: You are a Database Administrator and Data Analyst. Optimize relational schemas, write queries, analyze indexes, explain execution plans, and write migrations.

setup: No setup required.

plugins.name: GitHub MCP
plugins.summary: Model Context Protocol server for managing GitHub repositories.
plugins.description: Connects the AI agent to your GitHub account to manage repositories, create issues, review pull requests, search code, and submit comments.
setup: Generate a Personal Access Token (classic) on GitHub with 'repo', 'workflow', and 'user' scopes.
Input the token during configuration to securely store it in your Aura vault.

tools.name: create_issue
tools.description: Create a new issue on GitHub.

tools.name: search_code
tools.description: Search for text across GitHub repositories.

plugins.name: Filesystem MCP
plugins.summary: Secure filesystem access for reading and writing files.
plugins.description: Gives the agent permission to perform file operations inside explicitly allowed directories.
setup: Specify an absolute directory path you want the agent to access.
The agent will only be able to perform filesystem tasks within that folder hierarchy.

plugins.name: PostgreSQL MCP
plugins.summary: Enables read/write database queries on Postgres.
plugins.description: Allows the agent to explore schemas, run queries, list tables, and analyze performance of a PostgreSQL database.
setup: Ensure you have a running Postgres database.
Input your connection string. Make sure the database user has limited/safe privileges if running in production.

tools.name: query
tools.description: Execute a read or write SQL query.

tools.name: describe_table
tools.description: Retrieve schema information for a table.

plugins.name: SQLite MCP
plugins.summary: Interact with local SQLite database files.
plugins.description: Enables querying SQLite databases, inspecting tables, and importing schemas directly.
setup: Provide the full path to a local SQLite database file.

plugins.name: Web Browser MCP
plugins.summary: Scrapes web pages with Puppeteer.
plugins.description: Launches a headless browser to access web pages, extract markdown/text, capture screenshots, and click links.
setup: Downloads and launches Puppeteer automatically. Requires active internet connection.

plugins.name: Playwright MCP
plugins.summary: Automate web tasks using Playwright.
plugins.description: Gives the agent capability to run web automation scripts, test frontends, and interact with single-page applications.
setup: Runs Playwright server inside a node instance.

plugins.name: Docker MCP
plugins.summary: Interact with local Docker containers.
plugins.description: Allows the agent to manage Docker containers, list images, inspect networks, and start/stop environments.
setup: Ensure Docker Desktop is running locally on your computer.
The agent will connect to the local Docker socket (/var/run/docker.sock or named pipe).

plugins.name: Memory MCP
plugins.summary: Simple graph-based local memory.
plugins.description: Enables semantic entity-relation graph storage to help the agent remember facts across conversations.
setup: Stores memory database in a local JSON file under your app data directory.

tools.name: remember_fact
tools.description: Add a new fact to memory.

plugins.name: Aura Dev Plugin
plugins.summary: An example plugin to kickstart local development.
plugins.description: Demonstrates plugin integration in Aura Work, highlighting tool bindings, assets, and custom settings integration.
setup: Open your plugin folder and run npm install.
Select the folder path inside the app to load it locally.

tools.name: sample_tool
tools.description: A simple dummy tool returning greeting messages.
